events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need evaluate scenario: commercial construction, flashing & WRB integration, observed field condition reverse-lapped WRB (upper layer behind lower). Work in-progress. Need sequence check. Reverse lapped means water can get behind lower layer, improper shingle lap. This is non-compliant and defective. Decision must be fail because dangerous or non-compliant. Risk high? Water intrusion potential, building envelope failure. Could be high. Work_stage in-progress. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 50? value not asked, 0. sound_source not audio, empty string. confidence 1.0. event_time 0. rate 0. order: sequence steps? The task asks evaluate procedure sequence and state what must happen before work can continue. Need include order list? order is for audio/video tasks when requested; maybe not relevant. But schema says order list of step-id. Could include proper sequence step IDs for flashing/WRB integration. But not required? We can include empty list or relevant. Since task asks sequence check, maybe include order of correct steps. But no step IDs provided. We can invent generic step IDs. workable list of activity IDs when task asks what work can still start. Not asked. Could leave empty. findings: reverse-lapped WRB, improper shingle lap, water intrusion risk. actions: remove/repair reverse-lapped section, install WRB with correct overlap (upper over lower), integrate flashing per manufacturer and IRC R703, inspect before covering. rationale. references: IRC R703, manufacturer instructions.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.text: {"decision":":fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"in-progress","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":50,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":1.0,"event_time":0.0,"rate":0.0,"order":["install-flashing-pans","install-WRB-bottom-up","overlap-upper-over-lower","seal-penetrations","inspect-before-cladding"],"workable":[],"findings":["WRB is reverse-lapped: upper layer is behind lower layer instead of overlapping it","Improper shingle lap creates a path for water to enter behind the WRB","Flashing-to-WRB integration is compromised and will leak"],"actions":["Stop WRB/flashing work in affected area","Remove or fold back the reverse-lapped WRB section","Reinstall WRB from bottom up so each upper course laps over the lower course","Integrate flashing with WRB per manufacturer details and IRC R703","Hold inspection before cladding or finishes cover the work"],"rationale":"A weather-resistive barrier must be lapped shingle-style with upper layers over lower layers and integrated with flashing to drain outward. Reverse lapping violates IRC R703 and manufacturer instructions, creating a high risk of water intrusion and concealed damage.","references":["IRC R703","Manufacturer WRB/flashing installation instructions"]}
